summary.intent: Fix three bugs discovered in pass 9 of a Codex-driven review of the file_upload state machine: drag-flag stranding on ClearFiles, auto_upload gating when accepted_count==0, and stale size limits in validation error messages.
summary.outcome: All three bugs fixed with TDD (151 tests passing, 0 uncovered lines, 90.07% crate coverage), spec updated to match, clippy clean, committed and pushed.

repo: Codex review passes can double-post duplicate threads for the same finding — de-duplicate by content before implementing.
The file_upload state machine lives in crates/ars-components/src/specialized/file_upload/mod.rs with tests in a sibling tests.rs.
Spec is colocated at spec/components/specialized/file-upload.md and has a validation step that must pass before commit.
The project tracks a per-crate coverage target; pass-9 left the crate at 90.07% with 0 uncovered lines.

code.finding: ClearFiles transition must clear drag flags before moving to Idle, mirroring the fix already applied for ku9/0CV.
auto_upload must gate on accepted_count > 0 before chaining StartUpload; previously it would start an upload even when all files were rejected.

RejectionReason::TooLarge and TooSmall now carry the size limit captured at rejection time, so validation_error_text uses the historically-correct limit rather than the current (possibly changed) ctx value.
Existing TooLarge/TooSmall assertions required updating after the enum variants gained a size-limit payload field.

workflow: TDD cycle: write failing tests first, then implement the fix, then verify 0 uncovered lines before committing.
Spec sync is a required step after every behavioural fix — the spec validator gates the commit.
Running clippy + fmt + coverage as a single compound command after tests is the standard pre-commit gate in this repo.

friction: Codex double-posted two of the five threads, requiring manual de-duplication before implementation.
Multiple sequential reads of the same file were needed to locate the right edit sites, suggesting the file is large enough that full-context reads are not always sufficient in one pass.
